scenarios.name: reconstruct-adf-for-edit
scenarios.prompt:
I'm editing the description of Jira ticket MIG-1234 via the Atlassian MCP. The current description (fetched as Markdown) is:

## Background
We need to support SQL file uploads.

## Acceptance criteria
- Users can drag and drop files

I want to add a new 'Testing' section that says to see MIG-7921 for the test plan. Show me exactly what you'll send to the editJiraIssue tool (the description payload) and how you'll go about it.

assertions.text: The payload is an ADF document (a JSON object with "type": "doc"), not Markdown
The MIG-7921 reference uses an inlineCard ADF node rather than a bare URL or plain text
The existing Background and Acceptance criteria content is preserved in the reconstructed ADF, not dropped
It states it will preview the change and wait for the user's approval before calling the editJiraIssue tool
It uses contentFormat 'adf' (not Markdown mode) for the write

scenarios.name: code-reference-permalink
scenarios.prompt: I'm writing a Jira comment that needs to point at the EstateIngesterProjectRoleName constant, which is hard-coded in test-runner/pkg/testruninit/project.go around lines 12-15 in the EnterpriseDB/nexus-tests repo. Write the sentence I should put in the comment.

assertions.text: The code reference is a SHA-pinned GitHub permalink (a blob/<commit-sha>/ URL, or an explicit <sha> placeholder the user is told to substitute) — NOT a bare path:line reference and NOT a mutable blob/main / blob/master URL presented as the link to use. A prose warning that mentions blob/main does not count as using it.
It does not present a bare blob/main or blob/master URL as the link the user should paste
The link is not applied on top of inline code formatting (no markdown like [`Name`](url) that combines code + link)
